task_completion.description: Verify the initial Tor exit IP address is recorded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/ip_before.txt containing a valid public IP address, or a command (like curl https://check.torproject.org/) redirecting output to this file.

task_completion.description: Verify the Python script is created at the correct path and connects to the correct port with cookie authentication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/tor_control.py. The code must include a connection to 127.0.0.1 on port 9151 and logic to authenticate using the Tor session's authentication cookie.

task_completion.description: Verify the script contains logic to extract the Tor daemon version and save it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code in /home/ga/Documents/tor_control.py. It must contain commands to request the Tor version (e.g., 'GETINFO version') and write the response to /home/ga/Documents/tor_version.txt.

task_completion.description: Verify the script contains logic to trigger a new routing identity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code in /home/ga/Documents/tor_control.py. It must contain a command to send the 'SIGNAL NEWNYM' signal to the Tor control port.

task_completion.description: Verify the Tor version was successfully extracted and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/tor_version.txt. It should contain a valid Tor version string (e.g., 'Tor 0.4.x.x').

task_completion.description: Verify the post-rotation Tor exit IP address is recorded and differs from the initial IP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/ip_after.txt containing a valid public IP address. This IP address must be visibly different from the IP address recorded in ip_before.txt.

integrity.description: Ensure the Python script was actually executed to generate the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal history showing 'python3 /home/ga/Documents/tor_control.py' (or similar execution command) rather than the user manually creating tor_version.txt using 'echo' or a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the IP addresses were genuinely fetched from the specified URL.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of 'curl' or 'wget' commands targeting 'https://check.torproject.org/' (or a script doing the same) in the terminal history, ensuring the IPs weren't just randomly typed.

integrity.description: Ensure the script does not contain hardcoded outputs for the version or IPs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the script source code to ensure it dynamically fetches the version from the control port and doesn't just write a hardcoded string to tor_version.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Python script must be located at /home/ga/Documents/tor_control.py and connect to the Tor Browser Control Port on 9151. The extracted Tor version must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/tor_version.txt. The initial IP address must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/ip_before.txt, and the IP address after the NEWNYM signal must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/ip_after.txt.
